Alloys with FCC-dominated microstructures, such as Al16.67-Co16.67-Cr16.67-Fe16.67-Ni33.32 (81.7% FCC), processed via arc melting with four remelts and water quenching, form discontinuous Al₂O₃/Cr₂O₃ layers with spinels, resulting in reduced oxidation rate (~10⁻⁹ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹) but poor scale adhesion (specific weight change of -0.07 mg/cm² after 10 cycles) due to FCC phase limiting continuous Al₂O₃ formation and promoting Cr₂O₃ spallation. Y/Hf-doped alloys like Al22.42857-Co20.38776-Cr16.3102-Fe20.38776-Hf0.05-Ni20.38776-Y0.05 achieve exceptional oxidation resistance through a coherent dual-phase nanostructure (A2/B2) stabilized by arc-melting, drop-casting, remelting, and prolonged heat treatment. rag_summary.**MCrAlY alloy Ni39.836Cr17.9262Co17.926: The NiCoCrAlY-based alloys (Al23.32-Co19.64-Cr16.46-Ni40.3-Y0.28, Al23.0-Co18.83-Cr16.2-Ni41.69-Y0.28, Al22.5-Co19.7-Cr16.5-Fe0.0-Hf0.05-Ni41.3-Y0.05, Al22.37-Co19.71-Cr16.51-Hf0.05-Ni41.31-Y0.05, Al22.37-Co19.71-Cr16.51-Hf0.02-Ni41.37-Y0.02, Al31.45-Co19.55-Cr22.16-Hf0.01-Ni26.8-Y0.02, Al28.62-Co25.11-Cr21.04-Hf0.01-Ni25.21-Y0.01, Al25.0-Co25.0-Cr25.0-Ni25.0, Al15.375-Co15.375-Cr15.375-Fe15.375-Ni38.5, Al23.08-Co15.38-Cr15.38-Fe15.38-Ni30.78, Al16.12903-Co16.12903-Cr16.12903-Fe16.12903-Ni35.48387) exhibit oxidation performance critically dependent on processing-induced microstructures and resulting oxide scale mechanisms.
